LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EMA BELL'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFFS' UNOPPOSED MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT A NEW PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT EXECUTED BY ALL PARTIES AFTER THE REVISIONS TO SECTIONS 3.2, 5.1, AND 14.1 WERE MADE AND AGREED UPON. THE PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED CONSENT JUDGMENT SUBMITTED PRIOR TO THE HEARING ARE REJECTED. PLAINTIFF TO FOLLOW THE COURT'S INSTRUCTIONS AND RESUBMIT A REVISED CONSENT JUDGMENT. JUDGE: RICHARD C. DARWIN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RCD)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Plaintiffs' unopposed Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and for Entry of Consent Judgment is GRANTED. Having reviewed the moving papers, the supporting declarations, and the revised Consent Judgment, the court finds that the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; the civil penalty amount is reasonably based on the criteria set forth In Health & Safety Code Section 25249.7(b)(2); and the attorneys' fees and costs awarded pursuant to the terms of the settlement are reasonable under California law.

Plaintiff is ordered to submit a new Proposed Consent Judgment executed by all parties AFTER the revisions to Sections 3.2, 5.1, and 14.1 were made and agreed upon.

The proposed order and proposed consent judgment submitted prior to the hearing are rejected. Plaintiff to follow the court's instructions and resubmit a revised consent judgment.

Judge: Richard C. Darwin, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RCD)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Continued To Jun-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-24-2025 Continued To Mar-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Consolidated Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's Motion To File Consolidated Complaint.

Plaintiff Ema Bell's Motion to File Consolidated Complaint is OFF CALANDAR.

A prerequisite to any such motion would be that the actions at issue have been ordered consolidated. No such order has issued here. Such an order would come only upon a properly noticed motion supported by evidence and a memorandum of points and authorities. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.113(a) ["A party filing a motion, except for a motion listed in rule 3.1114, must serve and file a supporting memorandum. The court may construe the absence of a memorandum as an admission that the motion or special demurrer is not meritorious and cause for its denial and, in the case of a demurrer, as a waiver of all grounds not supported."].) To be clear, the parties' agreement is not sufficient. If such an order is obtained, then the parties may seek leave to file a consolidated complaint.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
